name: Cooldown Indicator
description: A UI element that shows when an ability, item, weapon, or action is unavailable and when it becomes ready again.
design_notes: Cooldown indicators need to answer two questions quickly: can I use this now, and when will it be ready? The design should work at a glance during stress, support multiple input devices, and avoid relying only on tiny radial fills or color changes.
design_purpose: Communicate temporary unavailability without forcing players to memorize timers.
Help players plan ability rotations, defensive options, and resource timing.
Make failed inputs understandable when an action is still recharging.
player_fantasy: The player feels in control of timing because recovery state is visible and trustworthy.

parameters.name: action_ready_remaining_ms
parameters.description: Milliseconds remaining before the source action becomes available.

parameters.name: action_recharge_fill_style_id
parameters.description: Visual fill style for radial, bar, pip, or numeric display.

parameters.name: ready_flash_ms
parameters.description: Short highlight used when the action becomes ready.

parameters.name: numeric_countdown_threshold
parameters.description: Point at which exact seconds should appear.

parameters.name: unavailable_input_pulse_ms
parameters.description: Feedback duration after the player tries to use an unavailable action.

edge_cases: Cooldown resets, haste effects, pause, slow motion, and server correction can change the remaining time mid-animation.
Shared cooldown groups need one visible source of truth across multiple abilities.
Charge-based abilities should show partial readiness without implying all charges are restored.
Controller, keyboard, touch, and remapped inputs need glyphs that update while cooldown is visible.
common_bugs: UI countdown uses local time while gameplay cooldown uses simulation time and drifts during pause.
Cooldown fill reaches zero visually before the server accepts the action.
Ready flash plays after the ability was removed, swapped, or disabled by silence.
Color-only ready/unready states are unreadable for some players.
balancing_notes: Exact timers make optimization easier; vague fills preserve uncertainty and reduce UI pressure.
High-stakes defensive cooldowns need stronger feedback than low-impact convenience actions.
accessibility_notes: Use shape, icon state, numeric text, and optional audio rather than color alone.
Allow scaling or simplified cooldown displays when many abilities are visible at once.

implementation_notes.unity: Bind each widget to ability_id, cooldown_group_id, remaining_cooldown_seconds, and charge count from gameplay state. Drive ready flashes from cooldown-complete events, not animation timers.
implementation_notes.godot: Expose cooldown state as signals or a view model with ability_id, remaining time, max time, and charges. Control nodes render radial, numeric, or icon states from the same values gameplay uses.
implementation_notes.unreal: Feed UMG/CommonUI from ability cooldown tags, Gameplay Effect durations, or replicated ability state. Treat the server cooldown as authoritative and correct predicted UI when ability activation is rejected.
implementation_notes.web: Store cooldowns by ability_id and cooldown_group_id in simulation state. Render fill and numeric countdown from remaining time, and pulse unavailable input only after a rejected use event.

relationships.reason: Cooldown indicators need remaining duration, charge count, and authoritative ready state from cooldown timers.
Dash availability is easier to read when cooldown or charge state is visible near the action prompt.
Charge indicators and cooldown indicators can overload the same HUD space unless priority rules are defined.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses cooldown indicators across action bar abilities.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Shows ability readiness for combat actions.
